articles.title: SEC filing: DFAN14A — Fermi Inc. (DEFINITIVE ADDITIONAL MATERIALS)
articles.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer and affiliated entities have filed a definitive proxy statement with the SEC in connection with soliciting agent designations for a special meeting of Fermi Inc. shareholders. Neugebauer beneficially owns 146,516,035 shares of Fermi's common stock, and the filing advises all shareholders to read the proxy statement and other materials because they contain important information. The special meeting is to be held as promptly as practicable, although a specific date is not disclosed.

articles.title: SEC filing: DFAN14A — Fermi Inc. (DEFINITIVE ADDITIONAL MATERIALS)
articles.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer, co-founder and largest shareholder of Fermi Inc., has issued a press release responding to recent media inquiries and SEC filings, calling on the Fermi Board to maximize shareholder value and find a new leader to replace the nine senior leaders who departed after his termination. Neugebauer highlights his team's accomplishments during his tenure, including securing a 99-year ground lease and over $1 billion in financing facilities, and emphasizes the need for transparency and accountability in determining Fermi's future. The press release comes after significant positive stock momentum in response to Neugebauer's recent open letter, which outlined a dual path of tenant ac

articles.title: SEC filing: DEFA14A — Fermi Inc. (SOLICITING MATERIAL PURSUANT TO SECTION 240.14A-12)
articles.summary: Fermi Inc is urging shareholders to sign and return the WHITE Consent Revocation Card in response to former CEO Toby Neugebauer's consent solicitation to call a Special Meeting of Shareholders, citing Neugebauer's pattern of false claims and inaccurate statements. Neugebauer is seeking to replace the Board and leadership team with his own nominees, which Fermi's Board believes would jeopardize shareholder value and benefit Neugebauer and his affiliates at the expense of other shareholders. The company is highlighting its commercial momentum and progress under new management, and warning shareholders not to sign or return any GREEN Consent Cards sent by Neugebauer.

articles.title: SEC filing: DFAN14A — Fermi Inc. (DEFINITIVE ADDITIONAL MATERIALS)
articles.summary: Toby Neugebauer and his affiliated entities, collectively known as the Fermi Founder Parties, are soliciting shareholders to designate agents to call a special meeting of Fermi Inc. shareholders, and are providing additional information to street name shareholders on how to vote their shares. The Fermi Founder Parties, who beneficially own approximately 146.5 million shares of Fermi Inc. common stock, are seeking to call a special meeting to allow shareholders to hear proposals and make informed decisions. Shareholders who own shares through a bank, broker, or other nominee are encouraged to check their email and/or mailbox for solicitation materials and vote their GREEN voting instruction f

articles.title: SEC filing: DFAN14A — Fermi Inc. (SOLICITING MATERIAL PURSUANT TO 240.14A-12A)
articles.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer, along with other participants, has issued a press release reaffirming his support for John Sellers of Double Eagle as Chairman and CEO of Fermi, citing the need for enhanced accountability and credibility to prevent value destruction by the current Board and management team. Neugebauer claims that the current Board has failed to replace nine key senior executives who departed Fermi after his termination and has ignored proposals from large companies that could offer greater value to shareholders. The release also highlights Neugebauer's intent to continue his campaign to seat a new, strong, independent Board and restore Fermi's position as a pro-shareholder REIT.

articles.title: SEC filing: DFAN14A — Fermi Inc. (SOLICITING MATERIAL PURSUANT TO 240.14A-12A)

Neugebauer 2020 Trust, have filed a definitive proxy statement with the SEC to solicit agent designations for calling a special meeting of Fermi Inc. shareholders. The filing advises all shareholders to read the definitive proxy statement and other proxy materials because they contain important information regarding the solicitation.

articles.title: SEC filing: DEFA14A — Fermi Inc. (SOLICITING MATERIAL PURSUANT TO SECTION 240.14A-12)
articles.summary: Fermi Inc is mailing a Consent Revocation Statement to shareholders in response to a consent solicitation by former CEO Toby Neugebauer, who is seeking to call a Special Meeting of Shareholders. The company's Board of Directors urges shareholders to sign and return the WHITE Consent Revocation Card to protect their investment and support the company's continued execution of its Fermi 2.0 strategy. Neugebauer, who was terminated for cause, currently does not have the required support to call a Special Meeting, and the company emphasizes that it is making progress on its projects, including Project Matador, and is focused on maximizing long-term shareholder value.

articles.title: SEC filing: DFAN14A — Fermi Inc. (SOLICITING MATERIAL PURSUANT TO 240.14A-12A)
articles.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer and affiliated entities, collectively known as the Fermi Founder Parties, have filed a definitive proxy statement to solicit agent designations for calling a special meeting of Fermi Inc. shareholders. Neugebauer beneficially owns 146,516,035 shares of the company's common stock, while the other participants do not own any shares. The proxy statement and accompanying materials contain important information regarding the special meeting and are available on the SEC's website and upon request from the participants.

articles.title: SEC filing: DFAN14A — Fermi Inc. (SOLICITING MATERIAL PURSUANT TO 240.14A-12A)
articles.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer, the co-founder and largest shareholder of Fermi Inc., has filed definitive proxy solicitation materials to call a Special Meeting of Shareholders to elect a new Board majority committed to taking all actions to benefit shareholders. Neugebauer is seeking to replace the current Board with six independent directors who have no ties to the Neugebauer family or existing board dynamics, in order to consider a full-value strategic review and potential sale of the company. Shareholders of record as of June 10, 2026, are being asked to sign and return a GREEN agent designation card to support the call for the Special Meeting.

The participants, who collectively own a significant portion of Fermi Inc.'s common stock, with Toby R. Neugebauer beneficially owning 139,016,035 shares, are advising all shareholders to read the definitive proxy statement and other proxy materials as they become available. The solicitation is related to the filing of a definitive proxy statement on Schedule 14A and accompanying green agent designations card with the SEC.

articles.title: SEC filing: PRRN14A — Fermi Inc. (REVISED PRELIMINARY CONSENT STATEMENT)
articles.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer, co-founder of Fermi Inc., is soliciting shareholder support to call a special meeting to vote on expanding the board of directors and electing seven new candidates, including himself, and removing three current directors. The proposed changes aim to install a new board majority that would consider a potential sale or strategic partnership of the company, which Neugebauer believes could maximize value for shareholders. Neugebauer's effort is a second attempt to provide shareholders with a vote on the company's future after his initial attempt was obstructed by the company.
